                                                                      A - 4: Labor force by sex, age, and color



                                                                                              Total labor force                            Civilian labor force
                                                Sex, age, and color             Thousands of persons      Panic ipation rate   Thousands of persons      Parti cipation rate

                             For coverage of series, see footnote 1, table B-2.
                          2
                             Beginning January 1965, data relate to railroads with operating revenues of $5,000,000 or more.
                          3
                             Data relate to employees in such occupations in the telephone industry as switchboard operators; service assistants; operating room instructors; and pay-station attendants. In 1971, such
                    employees made up 29 percent of the total number of nonsupervisory employees in establishments reporting hours and earnings data.
                         4
                             Data relate to employees in such occupations in the telephone industry as central office craftsmen; installation and exchange repair craftsmen; line, cable, and conduit craftsmen;and laborers.
                    In 1971, such employees made up 34 percent of the total number of nonsupervisory employees in establishments reporting hours and earnings data.

                             Money payments only; tips, not included.

                         p= preliminary.

                           t As noted on page 7 of the January 1972 issue of this magazine, the series does not include earned pay withheld as a consequence of delays caused by the wage freeze in effect between Au-
                    gust 15 and November 14, 1971, and by administrative procedures of the Pay Board. These delays resulted from compliance with the Government's Economic Stabilization Program. Because these
                    withheld payments were substantial and fell due under a significant number of new labor-management contracts in that industry, they affected a large proportion of SIC 372. Accordingly, the series
                    will be supplemented by publication in the future of a series which will include withheld payments in the months earned.

                 COLLECTION                                                                    All national, State, and area employment, hours, earnings,
                                                                                           job vacancy, and labor turnover series are classified in accord-
                     Payroll reports provide current information on wage and               ance with the Standard Industrial Classification Manual, Bureau
                 salary employment, hours, earnings, job vacancies, and labor              of the Budget, 1967.
                 turnover in nonagriculturSl establishments, by industry and
                 geographic location.
                                                                                           Industry employment
                 Federal-State cooperation                                                     Employment data, except those for the Federal Government,
                                                                                           refer to persons on establishment payrolls who received pay for
                     Under cooperative arrangements with State agencies, the
                                                                                           any part of the pay period which includes the 12th of the
                 respondent fills out a single employment or job vacancy-labor
                                                                                           month. For Federal Government establishments, employment
                 turnover reporting form, which is then used for national, State,          figures represent the number of persons who occupied positions
                 and area estimates. This eliminates duplicate reporting on the            on the last day of the calendar month. Intermittent workers are
                 part of respondents and, together with the use of identical               counted if they performed any service during the month.
                 techniques at the national and State levels, insures maximum
                                                                                               The data exclude proprietors, the self-employed, unpaid
                 comparability of estimates.
                                                                                           volunteer or family workers, farm workers, and domestic
                     State agencies mail the forms to the establishments and               workers in households. Salaried officers of corporations are
                 examine the returns for consistency, accuracy, and com-                   included. Government employment covers only civilian em-
                 pleteness. The States use the information to prepare State                ployees; military personnel are excluded.
                 and area series and then send the establishment data to the                   Persons on establishment payrolls who are on paid sick
                  BLS for use in preparing the national series.                            leave (when pay is received directly from the firm), on paid
                                                                                           holiday or paid vacation, or who work during a part of the
                                                                                           pay period and are unemployed or on strike during the rest
                 Shuttle schedules                                                         of the period, are counted as employed. Not counted as
                     Two types of data collection schedules are used: Form BLS             employed are persons who are laid off, on leave without
                 790—Monthly Report on Employment, Payroll, and Hours;                     pay, or on strike for the entire period or who are hired but
                 and Form DL 1219—Monthly Report on Job Openings and                       have not been paid during the period.
                 Labor Turnover. These schedules are of the "shuttle" type,
                 with space for each month of the calendar year. The collecting
                 agency returns the schedule to the respondent each month so
                 that the next month's data can be entered. This procedure                 Industry hours and earnings

                   for all kinds of positions, classifications and employment, full           Size and regional stratification

                                                      Unemployment Insurance Data

                     Insured unemployment represents the number of persons               as a percent of average covered employment in a 12-month
                 reporting a week of unemployment under an unemployment in-              period ending 6 to 8 months prior to the week of reference.
                 surance program. It includes some persons who are working part          Initial claims are notices filed by those losing jobs covered by an
                 time who would be counted as employed in the payroll and                unemployment insurance program that they are starting a
                 household surveys. Excluded are persons who have exhausted              period of unemployment. A claimant who continued to be
                 their benefit rights and workers who have not earned rights to          unemployed a full week is then counted in the insured un-
                 unemployment insurance. In general, excluded from coverage              employment figure.
                 are those persons engaged in agriculture, domestic service, un-
                 paid family work, selected nonprofit organizations, State and              Because of differences in State laws and procedures under
                 local government and self-employment. Also excluded from                which unemployment insurance programs are operated, State un-
                 the insured unemployment count, but included as employed in             employment rates generally indicate, but do not precisely mea-
                 the household survey, are those persons who earned no wages             ure, differences among the individual States. Persons wishing to
                 during the payroll period because they were temporarily absent          receive a detailed description of the nature, sources, inclusions
                 from their jobs due to taking time off, illness and industrial          and exclusions, and limitations of unemployment insurance data
                 dispute as well as unpaid vacations. The rate of insured un-            should address their inquiries to Manpower Administration,
